era: Seattle, Washington. Noise/Experimental. E=6 -- MAXIMUM ENERGY. Third consecutive E=6 (107, 108, 109). Genre Stage 91. Self-released.

location.formative: Seattle's noise and experimental underground. No-Anchor operates at the extreme end of sonic intensity where punk, metal, noise, and experimental music collapse into a single point of maximum energy.
location.career: No-Anchor -- noise/experimental project. E=6 -- maximum energy. The collapse frequency as primary sonic language. Self-released. Bandcamp. Seattle.

associated_musicians.name: Hovercraft
associated_musicians.note: Both noise artists, both Seattle. Hovercraft is controlled noise/drone (E=4). No-Anchor is maximum-energy noise/experimental (E=6). Sustained abrasion vs. structural collapse.

associated_musicians.name: Earth
associated_musicians.note: Both in the noise/experimental space. Earth is drone metal (E=3, glacial). No-Anchor is noise/experimental (E=6, volatile). Geological time vs. detonation time.

associated_musicians.name: Veronica Lipgloss
associated_musicians.note: Punk/electronic (108) and noise/experimental (109). Two consecutive E=6 entries. The punk circuit combusts; the collapse frequency demolishes.

associated_musicians.name: Chong the Nomad
associated_musicians.note: Production (107), punk/electronic (108), noise/experimental (109). Three consecutive E=6. Maximum three-peat.

associated_musicians.name: Jimi Hendrix
associated_musicians.note: First E=6 in the engine. Four E=6 total: Hendrix (36), Chong (107), Veronica Lipgloss (108), No-Anchor (109). Maximum energy connects across 73 degrees.

associated_musicians.name: Kinski
associated_musicians.note: Both experimental Seattle projects. Kinski is krautrock/experimental (E=5, hypnotic). No-Anchor is noise/experimental (E=6, destructive). Sequential degrees.

theory_nodes.concept: The collapse frequency (one hundred tenth acoustic element -- the point at which musical structure collapses under the weight of sonic energy; riffs dissolve into feedback, rhythms fragment into chaos, frequency saturation, physical bass, maximum amplitude; E=6 structural demolition)
examples: No-Anchor (degree 109) -- noise/experimental. E=6. Structural collapse under maximum energy.
Contrast with the punk circuit (degree 108, Veronica Lipgloss) -- explosive but structured. The collapse frequency has no structure to preserve.
Contrast with the noise wall (degree 91, Hovercraft) -- controlled and sustained. The collapse frequency is uncontrolled and volatile.
Contrast with the glacial drone (degree 96, Earth) -- slow and meditative. The collapse frequency is fast and violent.
theory_nodes.description: The collapse frequency is the 110th acoustic element. Structural collapse under maximum sonic energy. E=6 demolition.

ancestors: The punk circuit (degree 108, Veronica Lipgloss) -- explosive. The collapse frequency is demolitive.
The noise wall (degree 91, Hovercraft) -- controlled. The collapse frequency is uncontrolled.
genealogy.relationship: One hundred ten acoustic elements across one hundred nine degrees. Track element 111 at degree 110.

theory_nodes.concept: The maximum three-peat (one hundred tenth artist-city pattern -- three consecutive degrees at E=6 maximum energy; 107 108 109; three different genres; E=6 transcends genre; unprecedented sustained maximum; the engine at redline for three straight degrees)
examples: Degrees 107-108-109: Chong the Nomad (Electronic/R&B), Veronica Lipgloss (Punk/Electronic), No-Anchor (Noise/Experimental). All E=6.
First sustained maximum in the engine's history. Prior to 107, E=6 appeared once (Hendrix, 36).
Three genres, one energy level. E=6 is an energy level, not a style.
theory_nodes.description: The maximum three-peat is the 110th artist-city pattern. Three consecutive E=6. Unprecedented.

ancestors: The maximum return (degree 108) -- E=6 returns after 72-degree gap. Now sustains for three.
E=6 established at degree 36 (Hendrix). One occurrence in 106 degrees, then three consecutive.
genealogy.relationship: One hundred ten artist-city patterns across one hundred nine degrees. Track pattern 111 at degree 110.

theory_nodes.concept: Noise/Experimental at Genre Stage 91 -- 91st genre stage; the most extreme genre to reach E=6; pure sonic demolition; self-released; distinguished from Hovercraft's Noise (91) and Earth's Drone Metal (96)
examples: Distinguished from Hovercraft (91, Noise -- instrumental/drone, E=4). No-Anchor is vocal, structured-then-collapsing, E=6.
Distinguished from Earth (96, Drone Metal -- glacial, E=3). No-Anchor is volatile and destructive.
The most sonically extreme genre to reach E=6 in the engine.
theory_nodes.description: Noise/Experimental at Genre Stage 91. New genre. E=6 maximum energy. Self-released. Seattle.

ancestors: Punk/Electronic (degree 108, Veronica Lipgloss, Genre Stage 90). Sequential genre stages.
Noise (degree 91, Hovercraft). Same abrasive territory, different energy.
genealogy.relationship: Genre Stage 91 at degree 109. Track at 110.

cross_references.relationship: NOISE LINEAGE! Both noise artists, both Seattle. Hovercraft is controlled noise/drone (E=4). No-Anchor is maximum-energy noise/experimental (E=6). Sustained abrasion vs. structural collapse.
CONSECUTIVE E=6! Punk/electronic (108) and noise/experimental (109). Two consecutive E=6. The punk circuit combusts; the collapse frequency demolishes.
E=6 THREE-PEAT! Production (107), punk/electronic (108), noise/experimental (109). Three consecutive E=6. Maximum three-peat. Unprecedented.
DRONE CONNECTION. Both noise/experimental space. Earth is drone metal (E=3, glacial). No-Anchor is noise/experimental (E=6, volatile). Geological time vs. detonation time.
E=6 LINEAGE! First E=6 (degree 36, rock). No-Anchor is the fourth E=6 (degree 109, noise/experimental). Maximum energy connects across 73 degrees and four genres.
